_comment: Empty adjectives / clichés that score as noise with both modern ATS (context analysis) and human reviewers. Single source of truth for TS + Python.

cliches.why: Everyone claims it; proves nothing.
Everyone claims it; proves nothing.
Recruiters' eyes skip it; show it via accuracy metrics.
Generic; demonstrate collaboration with a concrete example.
Assumed of any candidate; show output instead.
Assumed of any candidate; show output instead.
Overused; prove initiative with a result you drove.
Cliché; replace with a measurable achievement.
Tells, doesn't show; let outcomes convey it.
Empty adjective; demonstrate via impact.
Empty adjective; demonstrate via impact.
Claim, not evidence; show what you invented or improved.
Especially weak for senior roles; show board-level outcomes.
Vague filler with no information.
Cliché; replace with the actual record (numbers).
Tired cliché; describe the unconventional thing you did.
Tired cliché; describe the unconventional thing you did.
Cliché; show relationship or stakeholder outcomes.
Claimed by all; demonstrate via a communication result.
Claimed by all; demonstrate via a communication result.
Vague; show a time you ramped quickly with a result.
Vague; show a time you ramped quickly with a result.
Generic; show a problem you solved and the impact.
Without context it means little; show throughput.
Corporate jargon; say what you actually mean.
Cliché; show the responsibility and its result.
Empty corporate jargon.
Empty corporate jargon; quantify the value.
Self-applied, it undermines credibility.
Informal and unverifiable; state concrete expertise.
Informal and unverifiable; state concrete expertise.
Informal and unverifiable; state concrete expertise.
